title: Scripts and console

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Scripts and console; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Classic and module scripts execute JavaScript in browser realms with different scope, loading, and strictness rules.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A deferred classic script runs after parsing while preserving document order. Your goal is to attach and run a script, then explain source order and observable output. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: pf computing execution; html document skeleton. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Classic and module scripts execute JavaScript in browser realms with different scope, loading, and strictness rules. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Parser blocking, async, defer, module dependencies, and CSP change timing and availability. This draft therefore rejects: Every script tag executes immediately before the browser parses later HTML.

includes: Load scripts intentionally and use the console for bounded experiments rather than application output.
Attach and run a script, then explain source order and observable output.
excludes: Parser blocking, async, defer, module dependencies, and CSP change timing and availability.

examples.title: Scripts and console: concrete case
examples.situation: A deferred classic script runs after parsing while preserving document order.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A deferred classic script runs after parsing while preserving document order.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Choose explicit script loading semantics; defer waits for parsing, while async can execute as soon as fetched.
examples.result: The result follows because Choose explicit script loading semantics; defer waits for parsing, while async can execute as soon as fetched.
